_comment: data/menaion/schema.json — Menaion v1.0
CONTRACT: This schema governs all files under data/menaion/.
Every monthly file must conform to this structure.
SCOPE (v1): Fixed-date commemorations only.
- Movable feasts (Triodion, Pentecostarion) are not in this namespace.
- Octoechos weekday themes are not in this namespace.
- Only troparion/apolytikion text is stored in v1.
- Full stichera corpus is deferred.
RANK MODEL (v1 — four ranks only):
rank 1 — Great Feast / Feast of the Lord / Feast of the Theotokos.
Apolytikion always used; displaces Octoechos troparion entirely.
rank 2 — Polyeleos feast. Apolytikion used; Octoechos is not sung.
rank 3 — Six-stichera feast (Doxology). Apolytikion used.
rank 4 — Three-stichera commemoration. Troparion used after Octoechos.
SEASON-AWARE FEAST QUALIFICATION (live — engine v3.6/v3.8, verified 2026-03-13):
Outside Great Lent weekdays: ranks 1–4 qualify as feast overrides.
During Great Lent weekdays (Clean Monday through Great Friday): only
ranks 1–2 qualify. Ranks 3–4 fall through to the Lenten seasonal path.
This means ordinary rank 4 saint commemorations do not displace the
Lenten weekday office; major feasts (rank 1–2, e.g. Annunciation) still
override. This rule is enforced by HorologionEngine — it is NOT encoded
in this schema. The schema stores rank only; the engine applies the gate.
When multiple saints share a date, the highest-rank commemoration's
troparion is used. Ties resolved by the first entry in the array
(traditional precedence order).
TONE: Byzantine tones 1–8 (integer). Required when troparion is present.
MONTHLY FILE SHAPE: See monthShape below.
DATE KEY: MM-DD string (zero-padded month and day). Not year-dependent.
COVERAGE FIELD: Each monthly file carries a top-level _meta object
stating coverage status (imported_date_count, total_days, coverage_note).
Annual corpus COMPLETE as of v1.8 (2026-03-07). All 12 months imported.
Total commemorations ~370. Resolved: 369. One intentional hold: 05-18
(Venerable Peter of Caesarea in Cappadocia) — troparion_status: 'text-unavailable'.
TROPARION OBJECT: See troparionShape below.
A null troparion means: we know the commemorand exists but do not have
the troparion text yet — different from a date with no import attempt.
SOURCE EXPECTATION: All troparion texts should be drawn from published
Orthodox service books in the Antiochian/OCA Hapgood tradition,
or equivalent published liturgical translations (Jordanville, etc.).
Texts must not be fabricated. If a troparion is unavailable, the
troparion field must be null and troparion_status must be 'text-unavailable'.
GOVERNANCE: This file is the authoritative schema. Edits to the schema
require a version bump and a note in the monthly files' _meta.schema_version.
Do not add fields to monthly files that are not declared here.

_meta.coverage_note: string — prose summary of what has and has not been imported
_meta.imported_date_count: integer — count of date keys with at least one commemoration entry
_meta.total_days: integer — total days in the month

<MM-DD>.commemorations: array — ordered array of commemorationShape objects (highest rank first)

commemorationShape.name: string — primary liturgical name as used in services
commemorationShape.troparion_tone: integer 1–8 — required when troparion.text is present; null otherwise
commemorationShape.kontakion: OPTIONAL — null | kontakionShape. Absent key = not in scope for this commemoration.
commemorationShape.kontakion_status: OPTIONAL — 'resolved' | 'text-unavailable' | 'deferred'. Required when kontakion key is present.
commemorationShape.tradition_tags: array of strings — ['EOR', 'OOR', 'ANG', 'LAT', 'COE']. EOR = Eastern Orthodox. 'BYZC' commemorations use 'EOR'.
commemorationShape.notes: string | null — liturgical notes, alternate names, usage notes

troparionShape.title: string — e.g. 'Troparion of the Holy Archangels, Tone 4'

kontakionShape.title: string — e.g. 'Kontakion of the Nativity of Christ, Tone 3'

resolved.description: A commemoration with rank 1–4 and troparion text was found for this date. Text is returned.

not_imported.description: This date's month has not been imported into the Menaion corpus. A rubric is returned. NOTE: As of v1.8 (2026-03-07) all 12 months are imported; this code is no longer returned in normal operation. Retained for forward compatibility if future months are added or a month file fails to load.

no_commemoration.description: The date was imported but no commemoration with a troparion was found. Weekday theme troparion applies.

text_unavailable.description: A commemoration is known for this date but troparion text is not yet in the corpus.
